articles.title: About Horses
articles.content:
Horses come in a number of different breeds for different purposes and a myriad of patterns and colors. 

[container:tablebox-100]
[b][center]Size and Breeds[/center][/b]
[table]
[tr]
    [th]Great[/th]
    [td]
    	[li]18-22 hands, ca. 2,000 lbs. [/li]
      [li]Belgian and Shire: used for very heavy draft work normally, but occasionally trained for jousting and war
      [li]Value x 2.0 
      [li]SPD:3d6   STR:7d6   AGL:3d6   CON;6d6   INT:3d6   TMP:4d6 
    [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]Heavy[/th]
    [td]
    	[li]15-19 hands, ca. 1,750 lbs. [/li]
      [li]Suffolk, Clydesdale and Percheron (slightly smaller versions of the breeds above, but still capable of quite heavy draft work) 
      [li]Value x 1.7   
      [li]SPD:4d6   STR:6d6   AGL:3d6   CON:5d6   INT:3d6   TMP:4d6 
    [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]Medium[/th]
    [td]
    	[li]13-17 hands, ca. 1,400 lbs. [/li]
      [li]Thoroughbred, Mule, Arabian, Quarter horse and Morgan (mostly for riding or racing; draft work usually performed by teams, or with pack harnesses; frequently trained for war) 
      [li]Value x 1.5   
      [li]SPD:5d6   STR:5d6   AGL;4d6   CON:5d6   INT:3d6  TMP:4d6 
    [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]Light[/th]
    [td]
      	[li]12-16 hands, ca. 1,100 lbs. [/li]
        [li]Donkey, Burro, Barb, Hackney and Mustang (riding animals primarily; draft work uses pack harnesses, or team efforts for any larger wheeled vehicles - single animal vehicles are quite small) 
        [li]Value x 1.3   
        [li]SPD:6d6 STR;5d6 AGL:4d6 CON;4d6 INT;3d6 TMP:4d6 
   [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]Small[/th]
    [td]
      	[li]9-14 hands, ca. 850 lbs. [/li]
        [li]Shetland pony, Welsh pony, Dales pony and Highland Pony (riding animals for smaller people and races; draft work is generally small vehicles or light packs) 
        [li]Value x 1.0   
        [li]SPD: 4d6   STR: 4d6   AGL: 4d6   CON: 4d6   INT: 3d6   TMP: 4d6 
    [/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
The "hand" is the standard unit of measure for horses; it equals 4" (1" for each knuckle). A horse's height is measured on the fore shoulder, at the end of the neck (the withers). 
[/container]

Values for horses usually depend on the use to which they will be put. A good draft animal will average 75-100gp, a riding horse 50-80gp, and a decent war horse 350-500gp. These bases are modified by the following multipliers given for breed and quality. 

[container:tablebox-70]
[b][center]Adjustments for Quality[/center][/b]
[table]
[tr]
    [th][roll:1d100][/th]
    [th]Quality[/th]
    [th]Value[/th]
    [th]Adjustments [/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]01-05[/td]
    [td]Superb[/td]
    [td]x 10[/td]
    [td]SPD + [roll:2d4], AGL + [roll:1d8], CON + [roll:2d4] [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]06-15[/td]
    [td]Excellent[/td]
    [td]x 6[/td]
    [td]SPD + [roll:2d3], AGL + [roll:1d6], CON + [roll:2d3] [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]16-35[/td]
    [td]Good[/td]
    [td]x 3[/td]
    [td]SPD + [roll:1d4], AGL + [roll:1d4], CON + [roll:2d2] [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]36-75[/td]
    [td]Average[/td]
    [td]x 1[/td]
    [td]No adjustments [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]76-90[/td]
    [td]Mediocre[/td]
    [td]x .7[/td]
    [td]SPD - [roll:1d4], AGL - [roll:1d3], CON - [roll:1d3] [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]91-97[/td]
    [td]Poor[/td]
    [td]x .4[/td]
    [td]SPD - [roll:2d3], AGL - [roll:1d6], CON - [roll:1d4] [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]98-00[/td]
    [td]Cull[/td]
    [td]x .2[/td]
    [td]SPD - [roll:2d5], AGL - [roll:1d8], CON - [roll:2d5] [/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[/container]

Horses are rated for six requisites: SPeeD, STRength, STaMina, AGiLity, INTelligence, and TeMPerament. The physical requisites (SPD, STR, CON, AGL) depend on overall size, and vary in the number of dice rolled.
- SPD governs the horse's ability to (flat-out) run
- STR handles load carrying 
- HTK, CON controls endurance and healing
- AGL covers jumping and fighting ability. 
The base rolls for these requisites are given in the overall size descriptions above; they also depend on the quality of the animal involved, and adjustments should be made according to whatever level of quality is determined. 

[container:tablebox-60]
[b][center]Temperament Adjustments[/center][/b]
[table]
[tr]
    [th]Description[/th]
    [th]Effect[/th]
    [th]Breeds [/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Very Docile[/td]
    [td]+ [roll:1d8][/td]
    [td]Morgan, Barb, Hackney [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Docile[/td]
    [td]+ [roll:1d4][/td]
    [td]Clydesdale, Belgian, Ponies [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Stubborn[/td]
    [td]- [roll:1d3][/td]
    [td]Mule, Mustang, Percheron [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]High-strung[/td]
    [td]- [roll:1d6][/td]
    [td]Thoroughbred, Arabian [/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[/container]

The other two (INT, TMP) are mental requisites, and are relatively constant in number of dice rolled for original generation.
[ul]
[li]INT governs the horse's ability to learn and indicates how it will respond to training
[li]TMP measures the horse's reaction to people and other animals, the lower the number the meaner the horse (4 to 7 would be a real killer type, eminently suitable for a warhorse)
[/ul]

Some breeds tend to be slightly more docile, or more high-strung, than others, and receive an adjustment to TMP. 

[h2]Equine Coloration[/h2]
The color ranges for horses are as broad as the hair color ranges of humanity, with mixtures tossed in. Horses are either solid colors, slightly marked about the face and/or legs, lightly splotched with white (roans), or very thickly splotched with white (piebald, skewbald). Markings are small patches of white confined to the face and legs. These are usually remarked only on horses of otherwise solid color, but may occasionally occur on roans and balds. There are some special terms used for particular color combinations noted in the colors chart below. When rolling for areas on the well-marked horses, ignore further results of "roll multiple".

[container:tablebox-100]
[br]
[b][center]Roll for markings [roll:d100][/center][/b][br]
[table]
[tr]
    [th]01-25[/th]
    [td]Horse is a solid color, [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]26-38[/th]
    [td]Face marked [roll:1d100][li]01-12 bald-faced (all white)[li]13-41 blaze (large mark down face)[li]42-60 race (narrow mark down face)[li]61-82 star (small mark on forehead)[li]83-00 snip (small mark on muzzle) [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]39-43[/th]
    [td]Feet marked
    	[li][roll:1d4] for # feet/legs marked
      [li]For each marked leg [roll:d10]: 1-6 stocking (up to knee), 7-0 sock (around ankle) [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]44-55[/th]
    [td]Face and feet marked. Roll as above.[/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]56-75[/th]
    [td]Horse is lightly splotched with white [roll:1d100][li]01-09 : neck[li]10-18 : chest[li]19-30 : withers[li]31-45 : back[li]46-57 : flanks[/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]76-80[/th]
    [td]And also marked on face and/or feet. [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]81-97[/th]
    [td]Horse is thickly splotched with white [roll:1d100][li]01-06 neck[li]07-12 chest[li]13-20 withers[li]21-30 back[li]31-38 flanks[li]39-46 hindquarters[li]47-50 legs[li]51-76 roll twice (reroll 51+)[li]77-93 roll three times (reroll 51+)[li]94-00 roll four times (reroll 51+) [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [th]98-00[/th]
    [td]And also marked on face and/or feet. [/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[br]
[b][center]Roll for colouring [roll:d100][/center][/b]
[table]
[tr]
    [th]Solid[/th]
    [th]Marks[/th]
    [th]Roans[/th]
    [th]Balds[/th]
    [th]Color [/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]01-09[/td]
    [td]01-16[/td]
    [td]01-14[sup]a[/sup][/td]
    [td]01-19[sup]b[/sup][/td]
    [td]Black [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]10-15[/td]
    [td]17-22[/td]
    [td]15-22[/td]
    [td]20-25[sup]d[/sup][/td]
    [td]Dark brown [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]16-26[/td]
    [td]23-40[/td]
    [td]23-33[/td]
    [td]26-42[sup]d[/sup][/td]
    [td]Brown [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]27-34[/td]
    [td]41-47[/td]
    [td]34-37[sup]c[/sup][/td]
    [td]43-48[sup]d[/sup][/td]
    [td]Dark Bay [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]35-45[/td]
    [td]48-60[/td]
    [td]38-49[sup]c[/sup][/td]
    [td]49-60[sup]d[/sup][/td]
    [td]Bay [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]46-49[/td]
    [td]61-65[/td]
    [td]50-55[sup]c[/sup][/td]
    [td]61-68[sup]d[/sup][/td]
    [td]Blood Bay [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]50-54[/td]
    [td]66-70[/td]
    [td]56-59[sup]c[/sup][/td]
    [td]69-74[/td]
    [td]Liver Chestnut [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]55-65[/td]
    [td]71-84[/td]
    [td]60-71[sup]c[/sup][/td]
    [td]75-86[/td]
    [td]Chestnut [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]66-74[/td]
    [td]85-91[/td]
    [td]72-76[/td]
    [td]87-94[/td]
    [td]Golden Chestnut [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]75-79[/td]
    [td]92-95[/td]
    [td]77-83[/td]
    [td]95-00[/td]
    [td]Sorrel [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]80-85[/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td]Palomino [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]86-91[/td]
    [td]96-98[/td]
    [td]84-91[/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td]Dun [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td]92-97[/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td]Dapple Grey [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]92-97[/td]
    [td]99-00[/td]
    [td]98-00[/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td]Light Grey [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]98-00[/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td][/td]
    [td]Albino [/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[sup]a[/sup] referred to as “blue roan" [br] [sup]b[/sup] called "paint" [br] [sup]c[/sup] as "strawberry roan" [br] [sup]d[/sup] called "pinto"[br]
[/container]
